mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate and supports risk asset multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the easing path is intact.
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if 20-day SPX returns are negative or indistinguishable from zero in more than 60% of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS above recent trend indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which lifts near-term equity prices over the next 20 trading days through improved earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change turns positive and exceeds its prior 6-month average growth rate
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not outperform untriggered periods by at least 1 percentage point over 20 days, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 55% in any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening labor income and demand, which compresses risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when unemployment is accelerating.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of unemployment-spike episodes, or if the sign of the 20-day effect flips positive for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL is flat or falling, real activity is improving without a compensating inflation shock, which supports equities over the next 60 trading days through margin and growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and non-rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus INDPRO alone in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS tightens real financial conditions, which pressures equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation and high-policy-rate episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns, or if the interaction coefficient is not significantly negative in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals a housing demand slowdown that typically precedes broader cyclical weakness, making S&P 500 returns negative over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing contraction episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth weakens while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating in a way that undermines household income and equity earnings, producing negative returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is negative and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor deterioration signal does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered episodes have positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDPC1 trend with stable PAYEMS and low UNRATE reflects resilient final demand and labor income, which supports equity prices over the next quarter through earnings revision momentum.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is positive year over year while UNRATE remains below 4.5% and PAYEMS is not declining over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if soft-landing conditions do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if adding GDPC1 to labor conditions does not improve predictive accuracy versus labor conditions alone.
